titles.ar: باب فِي كَمْ يُعَادُ الْمَرِيضُ وَ قَدْرِ مَا يَجْلِسُ عِنْدَهُ وَ تَمَامِ الْعِيَادَةِ
titles.en: Chapter 6 – Regarding how many times to visit the patient, and for how long to sit in his presence, and the completion of the consoling

titles.ar: باب فِي كَمْ يُعَادُ الْمَرِيضُ وَ قَدْرِ مَا يَجْلِسُ عِنْدَهُ وَ تَمَامِ الْعِيَادَةِ
titles.en: Chapter 6 – Regarding how many times to visit the patient, and for how long to sit in his presence, and the completion of the consoling

segments: لَا عِيَادَةَ فِي وَجَعِ الْعَيْنِ وَ لَا تَكُونُ عِيَادَةٌ فِي أَقَلَّ مِنْ ثَلَاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ فَإِذَا وَجَبَتْ فَيَوْمٌ وَ يَوْمٌ لَا فَإِذَا طَالَتِ الْعِلَّةُ تُرِكَ الْمَرِيضُ وَ عِيَالَهُ .
arabic.text: لَا عِيَادَةَ فِي وَجَعِ الْعَيْنِ وَ لَا تَكُونُ عِيَادَةٌ فِي أَقَلَّ مِنْ ثَلَاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ فَإِذَا وَجَبَتْ فَيَوْمٌ وَ يَوْمٌ لَا فَإِذَا طَالَتِ الْعِلَّةُ تُرِكَ الْمَرِيضُ وَ عِيَالَهُ .

segments: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ali Bin Asbaat, from one of his companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘There is no visiting regarding the eye sores, and a visitation does not happen to be in less than three days. So when it necessitates, so one day, and one day not (every other day). So when the illness gets prolonged, leave the patient and his family (to look after him)’.
en.hubeali.text:
A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ali Bin Asbaat, from one of his companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘There is no visiting regarding the eye sores, and a visitation does not happen to be in less than three days. So when it necessitates, so one day, and one day not (every other day). So when the illness gets prolonged, leave the patient and his family (to look after him)’.

segments: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ali ibn Asbat from certain persons of his people who have said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that visit is not necessary for one’s illness of eyes, also not before every three days, unless needed in which case it should take place with a one-day interval in between. If an illness prolongs, then the patient is left in the care of his family.”
en.sarwar.text: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ali ibn Asbat from certain persons of his people who have said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that visit is not necessary for one’s illness of eyes, also not before every three days, unless needed in which case it should take place with a one-day interval in between. If an illness prolongs, then the patient is left in the care of his family.”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/270)
gradings.label: ضعيف على المشهور
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/270)

narratorChain.raw: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَسْبَاطٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب فِي كَمْ يُعَادُ الْمَرِيضُ وَ قَدْرِ مَا يَجْلِسُ عِنْدَهُ وَ تَمَامِ الْعِيَادَةِ
titles.en: Chapter 6 – Regarding how many times to visit the patient, and for how long to sit in his presence, and the completion of the consoling

segments: الْعِيَادَةُ قَدْرَ فُوَاقِ نَاقَةٍ أَوْ حَلْبِ نَاقَةٍ .
arabic.text: الْعِيَادَةُ قَدْرَ فُوَاقِ نَاقَةٍ أَوْ حَلْبِ نَاقَةٍ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Abdullah Bin Al Mugheira, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The visitation is of a measurement of a suckling of a she-camel or milking of a she-camel’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Abdullah Bin Al Mugheira, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The visitation is of a measurement of a suckling of a she-camel or milking of a she-camel’.

segments: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ’Abd Allah ibn al-Mughirah from ’Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that duration of visiting a patient is as long as a baby- camel is given the chance to suckle the mother during milking the camel or as long as it takes to milk a camel.”
en.sarwar.text: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ’Abd Allah ibn al-Mughirah from ’Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that duration of visiting a patient is as long as a baby- camel is given the chance to suckle the mother during milking the camel or as long as it takes to milk a camel.”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/270)
gradings.label: حسن
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/270)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْمُغِيرَةِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب فِي كَمْ يُعَادُ الْمَرِيضُ وَ قَدْرِ مَا يَجْلِسُ عِنْدَهُ وَ تَمَامِ الْعِيَادَةِ
titles.en: Chapter 6 – Regarding how many times to visit the patient, and for how long to sit in his presence, and the completion of the consoling

segments: حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو زَيْدٍ قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي مَوْلًى لِجَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ مَرِضَ بَعْضُ مَوَالِيهِ فَخَرَجْنَا إِلَيْهِ نَعُودُهُ وَ نَحْنُ عِدَّةٌ مِنْ مَوَالِي جَعْفَرٍ فَاسْتَقْبَلَنَا جَعْفَرٌ ( عليه السلام ) فِي بَعْضِ الطَّرِيقِ فَقَالَ لَنَا أَيْنَ تُرِيدُونَ فَقُلْنَا نُرِيدُ فُلَاناً نَعُودُهُ فَقَالَ لَنَا قِفُوا فَوَقَفْنَا فَقَالَ مَعَ أَحَدِكُمْ تُفَّاحَةٌ أَوْ سَفَرْجَلَةٌ أَوْ أُتْرُجَّةٌ أَوْ لُعْقَةٌ مِنْ طِيبٍ أَوْ قِطْعَةٌ مِنْ عُودِ بَخُورٍ فَقُلْنَا مَا مَعَنَا شَيْ‏ءٌ مِنْ هَذَا فَقَالَ أَ مَا تَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ الْمَرِيضَ يَسْتَرِيحُ إِلَى كُلِّ مَا أُدْخِلَ بِهِ عَلَيْهِ .
arabic.text: حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو زَيْدٍ قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي مَوْلًى لِجَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ مَرِضَ بَعْضُ مَوَالِيهِ فَخَرَجْنَا إِلَيْهِ نَعُودُهُ وَ نَحْنُ عِدَّةٌ مِنْ مَوَالِي جَعْفَرٍ فَاسْتَقْبَلَنَا جَعْفَرٌ ( عليه السلام ) فِي بَعْضِ الطَّرِيقِ فَقَالَ لَنَا أَيْنَ تُرِيدُونَ فَقُلْنَا نُرِيدُ فُلَاناً نَعُودُهُ فَقَالَ لَنَا قِفُوا فَوَقَفْنَا فَقَالَ مَعَ أَحَدِكُمْ تُفَّاحَةٌ أَوْ سَفَرْجَلَةٌ أَوْ أُتْرُجَّةٌ أَوْ لُعْقَةٌ مِنْ طِيبٍ أَوْ قِطْعَةٌ مِنْ عُودِ بَخُورٍ فَقُلْنَا مَا مَعَنَا شَيْ‏ءٌ مِنْ هَذَا فَقَالَ أَ مَا تَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ الْمَرِيضَ يَسْتَرِيحُ إِلَى كُلِّ مَا أُدْخِلَ بِهِ عَلَيْهِ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Musa Bin Al Hassan, from Al Fazl Bin Aamir Abu Al Abbas, from Musa Bin Al Qasim who said,

‘Abu Zayd narrated to me saying, ‘A slave of Ja’far Bin Muhammad<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘One of the ones in his<sup>asws</sup> Wilayah became sick, so we went out to visit him, and we were a number of the ones in the Wilayah of Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>. So we met Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> in one of the streets. He<sup>asws</sup> said to us: ‘Where are you intending (to go to)?’ So we said, ‘We are intending to visit so and so’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said to us: ‘Pause’. So we paused. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Is there with one of you, an apple, or a quince, or a citron, or a spoonful of perfume, or a piece of incense fragrance?’ So we said, ‘There is nothing from this with us’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Are you not knowing that the patient is relieved to see what everyone comes over to him with?’
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Musa Bin Al Hassan, from Al Fazl Bin Aamir Abu Al Abbas, from Musa Bin Al Qasim who said,

‘Abu Zayd narrated to me saying, ‘A slave of Ja’far Bin Muhammad<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘One of the ones in his<sup>asws</sup> Wilayah became sick, so we went out to visit him, and we were a number of the ones in the Wilayah of Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>. So we met Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> in one of the streets. He<sup>asws</sup> said to us: ‘Where are you intending (to go to)?’ So we said, ‘We are intending to visit so and so’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said to us: ‘Pause’. So we paused. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Is there with one of you, an apple, or a quince, or a citron, or a spoonful of perfume, or a piece of incense fragrance?’ So we said, ‘There is nothing from this with us’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Are you not knowing that the patient is relieved to see what everyone comes over to him with?’

segments: 3. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Musa ibn al-Hassan from al-Fadl ibn ‘Amir abu al-‘Abbass from Musa ibn al-Qasim who has said that abu Zayd said to me the following: “A servant of Ja‘far ibn Muhammad, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, informed me that once one of the followers of the Imam became ill and we went to visit him. We were a group of the followers of Ja‘far ibn Muhammad, ‘Alayhi al-Salam. He (the Imam) met us at a distance on the way and asked, ‘Where are you going?’ We replied, ‘We like to visit so and so patient.’ He said, ‘Wait.’ We waited. He asked, ‘Is there with any of you some apple, pears, peaches or a measure of perfumes or fragrance like ‘Ud or bakhur?’ We replied, ‘We do not have any such things with us.’ The Imam then said, ‘You should know that a patient feels relieved when something is presented to him.’”
en.sarwar.text: 3. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Musa ibn al-Hassan from al-Fadl ibn ‘Amir abu al-‘Abbass from Musa ibn al-Qasim who has said that abu Zayd said to me the following: “A servant of Ja‘far ibn Muhammad, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, informed me that once one of the followers of the Imam became ill and we went to visit him. We were a group of the followers of Ja‘far ibn Muhammad, ‘Alayhi al-Salam. He (the Imam) met us at a distance on the way and asked, ‘Where are you going?’ We replied, ‘We like to visit so and so patient.’ He said, ‘Wait.’ We waited. He asked, ‘Is there with any of you some apple, pears, peaches or a measure of perfumes or fragrance like ‘Ud or bakhur?’ We replied, ‘We do not have any such things with us.’ The Imam then said, ‘You should know that a patient feels relieved when something is presented to him.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/271)
gradings.label: مجهول
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/271)

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنِ الْفَضْلِ بْنِ عَامِرٍ أَبِي الْعَبَّاسِ عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ الْقَاسِمِ قَالَ

titles.ar: باب فِي كَمْ يُعَادُ الْمَرِيضُ وَ قَدْرِ مَا يَجْلِسُ عِنْدَهُ وَ تَمَامِ الْعِيَادَةِ
titles.en: Chapter 6 – Regarding how many times to visit the patient, and for how long to sit in his presence, and the completion of the consoling

segments: تَمَامُ الْعِيَادَةِ لِلْمَرِيضِ أَنْ تَضَعَ يَدَكَ عَلَى ذِرَاعِهِ وَ تُعَجِّلَ الْقِيَامَ مِنْ عِنْدِهِ فَإِنَّ عِيَادَةَ النَّوْكَى أَشَدُّ عَلَى الْمَرِيضِ مِنْ وَجَعِهِ.
arabic.text: تَمَامُ الْعِيَادَةِ لِلْمَرِيضِ أَنْ تَضَعَ يَدَكَ عَلَى ذِرَاعِهِ وَ تُعَجِّلَ الْقِيَامَ مِنْ عِنْدِهِ فَإِنَّ عِيَادَةَ النَّوْكَى أَشَدُّ عَلَى الْمَرِيضِ مِنْ وَجَعِهِ.

segments: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Muhammad Bin Suleyman, from Musa Bin Qadim, from a man,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The completion of the visitation to the sick is that you place your hand upon his forearm and hasten the arising from his presence, for the foolish visitation is difficult upon the patient due to his pain’.
en.hubeali.text:
A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Muhammad Bin Suleyman, from Musa Bin Qadim, from a man,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The completion of the visitation to the sick is that you place your hand upon his forearm and hasten the arising from his presence, for the foolish visitation is difficult upon the patient due to his pain’.

segments: 4. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Muhammad ibn Sulayman from Musa ibn Qadim from a man who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that visiting of a patient is complete upon placing your hand over his arm (praying) and standing up quickly to leave; an unwise visiting of a patient is harder for him than the pain due to illness.”
en.sarwar.text: 4. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Muhammad ibn Sulayman from Musa ibn Qadim from a man who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that visiting of a patient is complete upon placing your hand over his arm (praying) and standing up quickly to leave; an unwise visiting of a patient is harder for him than the pain due to illness.”

narratorChain.raw: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ قَادِمٍ عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب فِي كَمْ يُعَادُ الْمَرِيضُ وَ قَدْرِ مَا يَجْلِسُ عِنْدَهُ وَ تَمَامِ الْعِيَادَةِ
titles.en: Chapter 6 – Regarding how many times to visit the patient, and for how long to sit in his presence, and the completion of the consoling

segments: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) تَمَامُ الْعِيَادَةِ أَنْ تَضَعَ يَدَكَ عَلَى الْمَرِيضِ إِذَا دَخَلْتَ عَلَيْهِ .
arabic.text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) تَمَامُ الْعِيَادَةِ أَنْ تَضَعَ يَدَكَ عَلَى الْمَرِيضِ إِذَا دَخَلْتَ عَلَيْهِ .

segments: Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Al Hasan Bin Muhammad, from Sama’at, from someone else, from Aban, from Abu Yahya who said,
‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The completion of the visitation is that you place your hand upon the patient when you go over to him’.
en.hubeali.text:
Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Al Hasan Bin Muhammad, from Sama’at, from someone else, from Aban, from Abu Yahya who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The completion of the visitation is that you place your hand upon the patient when you go over to him’.

segments: 5. Humayd ibn Ziyad narrated from al-Hassan ibn Muhammad from Sama‘ah from more than one person from Aban from abu Yahya who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Visit of a patient is complete when you place your hand on him when you meet him.’”
en.sarwar.text: 5. Humayd ibn Ziyad narrated from al-Hassan ibn Muhammad from Sama‘ah from more than one person from Aban from abu Yahya who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Visit of a patient is complete when you place your hand on him when you meet him.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/272)
gradings.label: مجهول
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/272)

narratorChain.raw: حُمَيْدُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ سَمَاعَةَ عَنْ غَيْرِ وَاحِدٍ عَنْ أَبَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي يَحْيَى قَالَ

titles.ar: باب فِي كَمْ يُعَادُ الْمَرِيضُ وَ قَدْرِ مَا يَجْلِسُ عِنْدَهُ وَ تَمَامِ الْعِيَادَةِ
titles.en: Chapter 6 – Regarding how many times to visit the patient, and for how long to sit in his presence, and the completion of the consoling

segments: إِنَّ أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( صلوات الله عليه ) قَالَ إِنَّ مِنْ أَعْظَمِ الْعُوَّادِ أَجْراً عِنْدَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَمَنْ إِذَا عَادَ أَخَاهُ خَفَّفَ الْجُلُوسَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ الْمَرِيضُ يُحِبُّ ذَلِكَ وَ يُرِيدُهُ وَ يَسْأَلُهُ ذَلِكَ
وَ قَالَ ( عليه السلام ) مِنْ تَمَامِ الْعِيَادَةِ أَنْ يَضَعَ الْعَائِدُ إِحْدَى يَدَيْهِ عَلَى الْأُخْرَى أَوْ عَلَى جَبْهَتِهِ .
arabic.text:
إِنَّ أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( صلوات الله عليه ) قَالَ إِنَّ مِنْ أَعْظَمِ الْعُوَّادِ أَجْراً عِنْدَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَمَنْ إِذَا عَادَ أَخَاهُ خَفَّفَ الْجُلُوسَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ الْمَرِيضُ يُحِبُّ ذَلِكَ وَ يُرِيدُهُ وَ يَسْأَلُهُ ذَلِكَ

وَ قَالَ ( عليه السلام ) مِنْ تَمَامِ الْعِيَادَةِ أَنْ يَضَعَ الْعَائِدُ إِحْدَى يَدَيْهِ عَلَى الْأُخْرَى أَوْ عَلَى جَبْهَتِهِ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Haroun Bin Muslim, from Mas’ada Bin Sadaqa,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said that Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The visitation with the greatest Recompense in the Presence of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic is for the one who, when he visits his brother, lightens the seating (stays for less) unless the patient happens to love that (the prolonged sitting) and wants it, and he asks him for that’.
And he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘From the completion of the visitation is that the visitor places one of his hand upon the other or upon his forehead’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Haroun Bin Muslim, from Mas’ada Bin Sadaqa,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said that Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The visitation with the greatest Recompense in the Presence of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic is for the one who, when he visits his brother, lightens the seating (stays for less) unless the patient happens to love that (the prolonged sitting) and wants it, and he asks him for that’.

And he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘From the completion of the visitation is that the visitor places one of his hand upon the other or upon his forehead’.

segments: 6. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Harun ibn Muslim from Mas’adah ibn Sadaqah who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ’Amir al-Mu’minin, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Of the visitors who receive the greatest reward from Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, is one whose visiting time is short, unless the patient likes otherwise and asks him to stay for a longer time.’ He has also said, ‘A visit is complete when one places his hand over the other hand or upon his forehead (as an expression of concern).”’
en.sarwar.text: 6. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Harun ibn Muslim from Mas’adah ibn Sadaqah who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ’Amir al-Mu’minin, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Of the visitors who receive the greatest reward from Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, is one whose visiting time is short, unless the patient likes otherwise and asks him to stay for a longer time.’ He has also said, ‘A visit is complete when one places his hand over the other hand or upon his forehead (as an expression of concern).”’

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ هَارُونَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ مَسْعَدَةَ بْنِ صَدَقَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ
